description:
Add ~0.5-1s silent gaps between audio chunks to give breathing room. NO pause at the beginning — must engage immediately from the first frame. Add a ~1s silent pause at the END to prevent audio cutoff. Target total duration ~40-45s (up from ~32s). Generate silence with ffmpeg and insert between chunks in the concat pipeline.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No silence before first chunk (immediate engagement)
- [ ] ~0.5-1s silent gaps between each chunk pair
- [ ] ~1s silent pause at the end to prevent audio cutoff
- [ ] Total assembled duration ~40-45 seconds
- [ ] Pauses feel natural, not mechanical
- [ ] Concat pipeline updated to include silence segments

- Remotion scenes using shared components (BrandBackground, CodeBlock, TypewriterCode, CTA)
- Shared components extracted to assets/videos/shared/remotion/ (video 1 refactored to use them)
- Merged transition-solution pattern (no editor flash between chunks)
- Final 4K 9:16 mp4 under 60s with SRT subtitles
- Mono audio, bt709 color space, CRF 15 encode

description:

2. Add '## Issue Tracking (bd)' section to CLAUDE.md (bd quick ref, types, priorities, statuses, agent workflow, auto-sync rules)
3. Merge 'Landing the Plane' session-completion steps into /handoff command (quality gates, bd sync, cleanup, verify)
4. Remove AGENTS.md from CLAUDE.md structure comment
5. Delete AGENTS.md
6. Update any INDEX.md that references AGENTS.md

description: As a GHQ user, I want a well-designed deep research workflow so that the skill produces thorough, well-structured reports. Research ChatGPT deep research behavior. metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["Research flow documented: query → plan → search loop → synthesize → report","Markdown report template defined with sections and citation format","Iterative search strategy defined (how many searches, when to stop, how to follow leads)","Output path handling designed (user-specified path with sensible default)"],"e2eTests":["Design document exists and covers all workflow phases"]}

description: As a GHQ user, I want to run /deep-research with a query so that it autonomously searches the web, reads sources, and produces a comprehensive report. Create .claude/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md implementing the designed architecture. Include: query clarification phase, multi-step web search using WebSearch, source reading via WebFetch, iterative deepening when leads are found, citation tracking, markdown report generation, and save to user-specified path.

description:
Enhance run-loop to detect unblocked subtasks and execute them in parallel via multiple sub-agents, respecting dependencies and avoiding file conflicts

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running /run-loop on a task with independent subtasks executes them in parallel batches with max 5 concurrency
- Blocked subtasks wait correctly for dependencies to complete
- File overlap detection prevents conflicting parallel execution
metadata: {"goal":"Run-loop executes independent subtasks concurrently instead of sequentially","qualityGates":["qmd update"],"relatedSkills":["run-loop","execute-task"],"repoPath":"/Users/hassaansaleemmacbook2026/repos/ghq","successCriteria":"Running /run-loop on a task with independent subtasks executes them in parallel batches with max 5 concurrency, while blocked subtasks wait correctly","workMode":"worktree"}
title: Parallel execution of unblocked subtasks in run-loop

description: As a run-loop orchestrator, I want to build a dependency DAG from bd dep list for all subtasks and group them into execution levels (batches of independent subtasks) so that each level can run in parallel
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["Reads all subtask dependencies via bd dep list","Groups subtasks into levels where level N+1 depends on level N","Identifies which subtasks within a level are independent and can run concurrently","Handles edge cases: no deps (all parallel), linear chain (all sequential), diamond deps"],"e2eTests":["Given a task with 4 independent subtasks, all 4 are grouped in one batch","Given a task with A->B->C chain, each is in a separate sequential batch","Given diamond deps (A->B, A->C, B->D, C->D), batches are [A], [B,C], [D]"]}
title: Build dependency graph and identify parallel batches

description: As a run-loop orchestrator, I want to detect when subtasks in the same batch might modify overlapping files so that I can serialize them instead of running them in parallel
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["Uses subtask metadata (repoPath, scope, or description heuristics) to estimate which files each subtask will touch","When two subtasks in the same batch have overlapping file scope, one is deferred to the next batch","Logging indicates when a subtask was serialized due to overlap detection"],"e2eTests":["Two subtasks both targeting the same SKILL.md file are not run in parallel"]}
title: Detect file overlap to prevent parallel conflicts

description: As a run-loop orchestrator, I want to spawn multiple sub-agents in parallel (up to max 5) for independent subtasks in a batch, using Task tool with run_in_background, so that unblocked work proceeds concurrently
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["Spawns up to 5 sub-agents concurrently using Task tool with run_in_background","Each sub-agent runs /execute-task for its assigned subtask","Orchestrator polls/waits for all sub-agents in a batch to complete before proceeding to next batch","Handles sub-agent failures gracefully (marks subtask blocked, continues others)","Respects ONE loop at a time rule (no nested run-loops)"],"e2eTests":["Run /run-loop on a task with 3 independent subtasks — verify all 3 spawn as background agents simultaneously"]}
title: Implement parallel sub-agent spawning with max concurrency

description: As a run-loop orchestrator, I want to extend state.jsonl entries to track parallel batch execution so that the audit trail clearly shows which subtasks ran concurrently

title: Update state.jsonl schema for parallel execution tracking

description: As a run-loop orchestrator, I want the run-loop command file updated to replace the sequential while-loop with a batch-parallel pattern so that the full parallel execution flow is integrated
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["run-loop.md updated: section 5 loop changes from pick-one-execute to pick-batch-execute-parallel","Batch selection uses dependency graph from subtask 1","Overlap detection from subtask 2 applied before spawning","Parallel spawning from subtask 3 used for execution","State tracking from subtask 4 used for logging","Progress display updated to show N parallel subtasks in progress","Auto-reanchor runs after each batch completes (not after each subtask)","Context safety net counts batches, not individual subtasks"],"e2eTests":["Run /run-loop on a task with mix of independent and dependent subtasks — independent ones run in parallel, dependent ones wait"]}

description: Build the scheduler system that runs companies autonomously. The scheduler continuously checks for work, spawns Claude Code agents per company, handles failures and retries, and only escalates to the user via bd decision tasks. Includes strategy-based task generation, escalation policies, preference memory, and daily digests.
metadata: {"goal":"GHQ runs companies autonomously, only asking user for decisions","qualityGates":["shellcheck loops/scripts/*.sh"],"relatedSkills":["architect","backend"],"repoPath":"/Users/hassaansaleemmacbook2026/repos/ghq","successCriteria":"Scheduler dispatches agents for enabled companies, handles failures with retry, escalates via bd decisions, generates daily digests","workMode":"main"}
title: GHQ Autonomous Scheduler

close_reason: Draft status, decision type, and bd-resolve implemented and tested
description: Extend bd with: (1) new status 'draft' (◇) for tasks that exist but aren't ready to execute, (2) new issue type 'decision' for tasks requiring human judgment, (3) new 'bd resolve' command that closes a decision with a structured answer. bd ready must exclude draft tasks. Draft status slots before open: ◇ draft ○ open ◐ in_progress ● blocked ✓ closed ❄ deferred.
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["bd create --status draft creates a draft task","bd ready excludes draft tasks","bd create --type decision creates a decision task","bd resolve <id> --answer <text> closes a decision and records the answer","bd list shows ◇ symbol for draft status"],"e2eTests":["bd create test-task --status draft && bd ready --json returns empty","bd create test-decision --type decision && bd show test-decision shows type=decision","bd resolve test-decision --answer yes closes it with recorded answer"]}
title: Add draft status and decision type to bd

description: Add scheduler fields to companies/manifest.yaml (scheduler.enabled, scheduler.max_agents per company). Create .claude/scheduler.yaml with global scheduler config: max_concurrent_agents, cooldown_after_failure, daily_budget, blocked_hours. Update manifest schema docs.

title: Add scheduler config to manifest and scheduler.yaml

close_reason: Escalation policy system implemented with check-escalation.sh, example policies for both companies, and 13 passing tests
description: Create per-company escalation policy files at companies/{company}/policies/escalation.yaml. Define policy types: always_ask, autonomous, ask_once_then_remember, ask_until_confident (with confidence_threshold). Create a shell function or script that agents source to check escalation policy before taking action. When policy says must ask: agent creates a bd decision task (owner: user) that blocks the current task, comments context on the task, and exits.
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["escalation.yaml template exists with documented policy types","Shell function check_escalation reads policy and returns ask/autonomous","Example escalation policies created for both companies","Documentation explains each policy type and how agents use it"],"e2eTests":["escalation.yaml for each company is valid YAML","check_escalation function returns correct result for each policy type"]}
title: Implement escalation policy system

close_reason: Preference memory system implemented with write-preference.sh, read-preferences.sh, bd-resolve integration, and 12 passing tests
description: Create per-company preference files at companies/{company}/policies/preferences.yaml. When a user resolves a bd decision via 'bd resolve', the answer is automatically captured as a preference entry (question, answer, date, applies_to skill). Agents load preferences before executing and use them to avoid re-asking. Integrate with escalation policy's ask_once_then_remember and ask_until_confident types.
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["preferences.yaml schema defined with question/answer/date/applies_to fields","bd resolve writes to preferences.yaml automatically","Agents can read preferences before executing a skill","ask_until_confident tracks confidence count in preferences"],"e2eTests":["bd resolve writes preference entry to correct company preferences.yaml","Preference file is valid YAML after multiple resolve calls","Confidence counter increments correctly for ask_until_confident"]}
title: Implement preference memory system

description: Create loops/scripts/scheduler.sh — the core scheduler daemon. Reads manifest for enabled companies, checks for running agents via pid files (loops/agents/{company}.pid), writes lockfiles (loops/agents/{company}.lock) with task ID before spawning, ranks open unblocked tasks by impact x urgency x company_weight. Spawns one Claude Code agent per company running /run-loop on the top-ranked task — run-loop handles subtask orchestration, dependencies, and skill chains internally. The scheduler operates at the task level, not subtask level. One agent per company max. Designed to run via cron every 15 minutes.
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["scheduler.sh reads manifest and filters enabled companies","Checks pid files to skip companies with running agents","Writes lockfile with task ID before spawning agent","Ranks tasks by score (priority x urgency x company weight)","Spawns claude running /run-loop on the top-ranked task (not execute-task)","Respects blocked_hours and max_concurrent_agents from scheduler.yaml","Creates loops/agents/ directory structure"],"e2eTests":["scheduler.sh passes shellcheck","Dry-run mode lists what would be dispatched without spawning","Skips companies with scheduler.enabled: false","Skips companies with existing pid file for running process","Spawned agent prompt uses /run-loop not /execute-task"]}
title: Build scheduler dispatch loop

description: Create the strategy-to-task planner that runs as Phase 1 of the scheduler. Reads companies/{company}/strategy.yaml (goals, cadences, milestones, task_templates). Compares against existing bd tasks. Creates draft tasks to fill gaps (e.g. cadence says 2 videos/week but only 1 exists). Planner is idempotent — running it twice produces no duplicates. Spawns a lightweight Claude agent for goal decomposition. Draft tasks appear in digest for user review.
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["strategy.yaml schema defined with goals/cadence/milestone/task_template fields","Planner reads strategy and compares against existing tasks","Creates draft tasks only when gaps exist","Idempotent — no duplicate tasks on re-run","Example strategy.yaml created for at least one company"],"e2eTests":["Planner creates draft task when cadence gap detected","Planner creates nothing when all cadence slots filled","Draft tasks have correct parent epic and labels","strategy.yaml is valid YAML"]}
title: Build strategy planner for task generation

description: Add failure recovery to scheduler.sh. When a dead pid is found: read lockfile to identify task, check task status in bd. If task still in_progress (agent crashed): increment retry count in task metadata, move task back to open (up to 3 retries), then block and file decision for user after 3 failures. Log crash details. Clean up stale pid and lock files.
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["Scheduler detects dead pid and reads lockfile for task ID","Checks task status to distinguish clean exit from crash","Increments retry count in task metadata","Moves task to open for retry 1-3","Blocks task and creates user decision after 3 failures","Cleans up pid and lock files after handling"],"e2eTests":["Stale pid file with in_progress task triggers retry","Retry count increments correctly in task metadata","Third failure creates decision task assigned to user","Clean exit (task closed) just cleans up files"]}
title: Implement failure recovery and retry logic

description: Create loops/scripts/digest.sh that generates a daily markdown digest at loops/digests/YYYY-MM-DD.md. Reads bd state across all enabled companies. Sections: completed tasks, in-progress, blocked/failures, drafts needing review, pending decisions (with one-liner bd resolve commands). Scheduler calls this at end of day (configurable in scheduler.yaml). Digest should be scannable in 2 minutes.
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["digest.sh generates markdown file at loops/digests/YYYY-MM-DD.md","Digest grouped by company","Shows completed, in-progress, blocked, failed tasks","Pending decisions include context and bd resolve commands","Drafts section shows tasks needing promotion to open","Scheduler invokes digest at configured end-of-day hour"],"e2eTests":["digest.sh produces valid markdown","Digest includes all enabled companies","Pending decisions have correct bd resolve syntax","Digest file created at correct path with date stamp"]}
title: Build daily digest generator

description: End-to-end integration test of the full scheduler cycle: plan → dispatch → monitor → recover → digest. Create a test script that simulates: enabled company with open tasks, agent completion, agent crash with retry, decision escalation, and digest generation. Set up launchd plist (or cron) for the scheduler to run every 15 minutes. Document the full system in knowledge/ghq-core/scheduler.md.
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["Integration test script exercises full scheduler cycle","Test covers: dispatch, clean completion, crash recovery, decision escalation","launchd plist or cron entry created for 15-min scheduling","knowledge/ghq-core/scheduler.md documents the full system","Dry-run mode available for testing without spawning real agents"],"e2eTests":["Integration test completes without errors","Scheduler runs via cron/launchd and produces log output","Dry-run produces expected dispatch plan","Documentation covers all scheduler components"]}
title: Integration test and cron setup
